There are two zil here, one on either side of you. Both are males, as determined by their exposed, dangling genitalia. Their wings flutter incessantly behind them; they can’t fly in the enclosed space, yet the wingbeats keep their genitals at just the right height to waft their sweet musk in your direction. They do have almost head-to-toe chitin to protect them, some black, some yellow.
